name: Carl Allen
abstract: Carl Allen (born April 25, 1961) is an American jazz drummer. Allen attended William Paterson University.

interviews.title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Bobby McFerrin & Friends, plus The New York Giants

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I had a quartet. When I came back – who did I have in that quartet? Kevin Hayes on piano, Dwayne Burno on bass, and Carl Allen, drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I played years ago with Richard Davis, Carl Allen, Buddy Montgomery and myself and Ben Sidran for Ricky Lee Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Carl Allen was on the date on drums... Carl has been playing with me quite a bit recently
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: Carl Allen and I became best friends and we started working with Benny Green.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I called him up and said, “You want to come up and do some teaching up this way?” and he said yeah. He came up, and I gave him a job that would enable him to move up here.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Karl Berger
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. I recently did an interview with a drummer, Carl Allen, who said the classroom is the new bandstand.
about_quotes.doc_title: Karl Berger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014
